pr_body:
## Automated research run

**Topic:** onboard van-wert

Produced by `bosc research run` — read-only over the corpus. This PR adds only `data/research/` artifacts (findings + issue-proposal manifest); it alters no source bytes under `data/documents/**`. A maintainer review is required — the research App cannot approve or merge its own PR.

### Provenance
- model: `claude-opus-4-8`
- generated: 2026-06-28T20:28:47+00:00
- turns: 26 / cap 30
- tools: ToolSearch, mcp__watermark__program_overview, mcp__watermark__list_documents, mcp__watermark__list_extractions, Bash, Read, mcp__watermark__retrieve_corpus, mcp__watermark__hydrology_balance
- cost: $1.6299

### Proposed follow-up issues
- 5 opened (labels: `agent-proposed`, `needs-triage`)
- 0 skipped as duplicates of open issues

See `/Users/cparent/Code/goedelsoup/bosc/data/research/onboard-van-wert-2026-06-28/findings.md` and `/Users/cparent/Code/goedelsoup/bosc/data/research/onboard-van-wert-2026-06-28/manifest.yaml`.

issues.title: Ingest OEPA NPDES permit/fact sheet for Van Wert WWTP (OH0027910 / 2PD00006) and extract cited Town Creek 7Q10
issues.body:
## Background

The Van Wert WWTP permit `OH0027910` (`2PD00006`) is confirmed present in the ECHO reference inventory (`data/reference/echo/maumee-wwtp.all-npdes.yaml`), but `data/extracted/van-wert/` holds only `ONBOARDING.md` and `README.md` — zero ingested OEPA documents. The ECHO row carries `design_flow_mgd: null, design_flow_missing: true`, and the profile's `~4.0 MGD` figure (line 436 of `src/watermark/sites/_profiles.py`) is explicitly flagged `[open]` / uncited in-corpus.

Contrary to Lima's three ingested fact sheets (`2PH00006`, `2PH00007`, `2IG00001`) each with `source: document` 7Q10 values in `data/reference/hydrology/low-flow-7q10.yaml`, Town Creek appears in neither the cited nor the derived 7Q10 tables (`low-flow-7q10.derived.yaml`), and receiving-reach gauge `04191000` is absent from the derived set. This is the single most load-bearing omission for a site whose entire thesis is effluent dominance on a tiny tributary. Tracked as GH **#375**.

## Acceptance criteria

- OEPA NPDES permit or fact sheet for `OH0027910` / `2PD00006` is ingested into `data/documents/van-wert/` (or the canonical documents path).
- A structured extraction is committed under `data/extracted/van-wert/` referencing that document.
- `data/reference/hydrology/low-flow-7q10.yaml` gains a Town Creek entry with `source: document` and confidence ≥ medium.
- Design flow is recorded as a cited value (not `null`) and the `~4.0 MGD` profile comment is either confirmed or corrected with a document citation.
- `ONBOARDING.md` gate box for permit ingestion is checked.

**Rationale:** Without a cited Town Creek 7Q10 derived from the actual permit, every 'effluent-dominated' claim for van-wert remains an unverified inference, directly blocking promotion to `selectable: true`.

---
_Proposed by `bosc research run` (/Users/cparent/Code/goedelsoup/bosc/data/research/onboard-van-wert-2026-06-28); inert until a maintainer triages it._
<!-- research-dedupe: ingest-oepa-npdes-permit-fact-sheet-for-van-wert-wwtp-oh0027910 -->

issues.title: Re-run basin-screen for OH0027910 against Town Creek 7Q10 and record the dilution ratio
issues.body:
## Background

The findings note that `basin-screen` ran across only 7 of 129 dischargers and that `OH0027910` was either not among them or was screened against a mainstem proxy that overstates dilution by orders of magnitude (`ONBOARDING.md` line 22). Town Creek does not appear in `low-flow-7q10.derived.yaml`, so any prior screen of the Van Wert WWTP used a receiving-water flow value that is not site-specific. Until `OH0027910` is screened against Town Creek's own 7Q10, the site's core thesis — effluent dominance on a small tributary — is `[inference]`, not a documented finding. Tracked as GH **#376**.

This task is downstream of #375 (permit ingest + 7Q10 extraction) but should be filed and tracked independently.

## Acceptance criteria

- `basin-screen` is re-run with `OH0027910` included and Town Creek 7Q10 as the receiving-water denominator.
- The resulting dilution ratio (effluent design flow ÷ Town Creek 7Q10) is recorded in a committed artifact (e.g., updated `ONBOARDING.md` or a new screen results file).
- The screen result is cited as the basis for any 'effluent-dominated' characterization in site documentation.
- `ONBOARDING.md` gate box for basin-screen coverage is checked.

**Rationale:** A site whose promotional narrative centers on effluent dominance cannot be responsibly advanced until the dilution ratio is computed from the correct receiving-water flow, not a mainstem proxy.

---
_Proposed by `bosc research run` (/Users/cparent/Code/goedelsoup/bosc/data/research/onboard-van-wert-2026-06-28); inert until a maintainer triages it._
<!-- research-dedupe: re-run-basin-screen-for-oh0027910-against-town-creek-7q10-and-re -->

issues.title: Obtain a Van-Wert-jurisdiction primary document for QTS data-center activity or commit a formal negative-search record
issues.body:
## Background

The findings confirm that all data-center activity for van-wert is documented **secondhand through Allen-County instruments**. The QTS `$10B Van Wert County campus` claim (up to 4,500 construction jobs, closed-loop cooling ~600,000 gal/yr) derives solely from proponent testimony in `data/extracted/legal/select-committee-2026/witness-submissions.digest.yaml` — flagged `[verified]` only that the claim appears in the record, not that the figures are independently confirmed. No Van-Wert-jurisdiction primary instrument (county/city ED filing, CRA/PILOT agreement, recorder deed, or utility interconnection application) exists in the corpus. Tracked as GH **#377**.

The findings also warn explicitly: *do not import the Allen-County Bistrozzi/Montfort/Ziance graph into van-wert without a filed bridge.*

## Acceptance criteria

- Either: a Van-Wert-jurisdiction primary document (recorder instrument, CRA/PILOT resolution, county commissioner minutes, or utility filing naming Van Wert County) is ingested and a structured extraction committed to `data/extracted/van-wert/`;
- Or: a formal negative-search record is committed to `ONBOARDING.md` documenting the sources searched, dates, and null result.
- The $10B / 4,500-jobs figures are annotated with their evidential status (proponent testimony vs. documented instrument) in all site-facing files.

**Rationale:** Promotion of van-wert with a data-center thesis resting entirely on proponent legislative testimony — and zero jurisdiction-specific primary documents — would misrepresent the evidential basis of the site's economic rationale.

---
_Proposed by `bosc research run` (/Users/cparent/Code/goedelsoup/bosc/data/research/onboard-van-wert-2026-06-28); inert until a maintainer triages it._
<!-- research-dedupe: obtain-a-van-wert-jurisdiction-primary-document-for-qts-data-cen -->

issues.title: Resolve whether QTS and Thor Equities name the same Van Wert data-center project
issues.body:
## Background

The findings surface two distinct data-center threads pointing at Van Wert County:
1. **QTS** — `$10B` campus, documented in `data/extracted/legal/select-committee-2026/witness-submissions.digest.yaml` (proponent testimony, Allen-County select-committee record).
2. **Thor Equities** — *"also doing a Van Wert data center; brought by AEP,"* 1-yr LOI dated 2025-03-27 at `$50K/ac` on Perry Industrial Park (Allen County), documented in `data/extracted/aedg/paac-board-minutes.minutes.yaml`.

The witness digest *flags* a possible tie between the two (line 120), but no instrument in the corpus establishes that they are the same project. The findings explicitly warn: *"Do not merge"* and *"do not import the Allen-County … graph into van-wert without a filed bridge."* Tracked as GH **#378**.

## Acceptance criteria

- A primary document (recorder deed, utility application, press release with legal entity names, or official county filing) is located that either:
  - Confirms QTS and Thor Equities refer to the same project (with the corporate relationship cited), or
  - Establishes them as distinct projects with separate parcels/permits.
- The corpus entries for both threads are updated to reflect the determination, with citations.
- If no resolution is found after a documented search, a negative-search note is added to `ONBOARDING.md` and the two entities remain in separate register entries.

**Rationale:** Merging two potentially distinct multi-billion-dollar projects on the basis of an uncorroborated digest flag would corrupt the site's entity graph and inflate activity estimates.

---
_Proposed by `bosc research run` (/Users/cparent/Code/goedelsoup/bosc/data/research/onboard-van-wert-2026-06-28); inert until a maintainer triages it._
<!-- research-dedupe: resolve-whether-qts-and-thor-equities-name-the-same-van-wert-dat -->

issues.title: File a GIS tracking issue for Van Wert County PAT MapServer TLS failure and parcel/zoning fallback path
issues.body:
## Background

The findings note that `parcels_url` and `zoning_url` are both `"TODO"` in `src/watermark/sites/_profiles.py` (lines 459–465), and `gis_parcel` / `gis_zoning` are both `None`. The Van Wert County PAT MapServer (`ags.bhamaps.com`, Bruce Harris & Assoc.) was identified as the likely source but has an **expired TLS certificate**, making it unconsuable by `cached_get`/httpx. The findings explicitly state: *"do not weaken TLS for it."* No City of Van Wert zoning REST catalog was found. The parcel/footprint absence also blocks the coupled SSURGO HSG confirmation (`dominant_hsg: "D"` remains `[inference]`), since HSG requires an area-weighted footprint.

This gap is currently tracked only in `ONBOARDING.md` with no dedicated GitHub issue.

## Acceptance criteria

- A GitHub issue exists (this one) tracking the TLS/parcel/zoning gap with the MapServer URL and the TLS expiry noted.
- A fallback path is documented: either the Van Wert County Engineer's-office parcel shapefile (direct download) or an alternative REST endpoint.
- Once a valid parcel source is confirmed, `parcels_url` and `gis_parcel` in `_profiles.py` are populated and a `GisParcelSchema` entry committed.
- City of Van Wert zoning layer is either registered or formally accepted as map-only with a note in `ONBOARDING.md`.
- SSURGO HSG confirmation is unblocked (footprint geometry present) and `dominant_hsg` upgraded from `[inference]` to a cited area-weighted value.

**Rationale:** The parcel/footprint gap cascades into both the SSURGO HSG inference and the absence of any site-boundary geometry, which are prerequisites for credible water-balance and land-use analysis.

---
_Proposed by `bosc research run` (/Users/cparent/Code/goedelsoup/bosc/data/research/onboard-van-wert-2026-06-28); inert until a maintainer triages it._
<!-- research-dedupe: file-a-gis-tracking-issue-for-van-wert-county-pat-mapserver-tls -->
